title: Connections, Channel Ownership, Heartbeats, and Recovery · Execution Trace: Recovery Order Generation
summary: A Go 1.26 service team must assign one recovery owner, reject stale generations, drain notifications, and test amqp091-go 1.12 recovery limits; reviewers reject hidden goroutine ownership, unbounded work, stale channel state, and claims that stop at a nil client error. The learner predicts values, ownership transitions, and stop paths before seeing a model. Planned artifact: Add and verify the recovery order generation increment in a cancellable connection and channel recovery state machine. Le…
objectives: Recover connection, channels, topology, confirm state, prefetch, consumers, and publisher admission in generation order while rejecting stale notifications and deliveries.
Reconnect with bounded exponential backoff, jitter, cancellation, credential refresh, readiness, and a single recovery owner; in Go, retain ownership, stop-path, and changed-case evidence.
Preserve previously learned constraints and attach observable changed-case evidence.

steps.title: RabbitMQ and Go Predict Recovery Order Generation · Theory Recovery Order Generation
steps.instruction:
theory case — A theory Go messaging team handles case 5843 during consumer shutdown drill with one blocked handler goroutine. Build deterministic Go evidence to bound heartbeat and recovery delay while rejecting stale channel-generation notifications; keep credentials and connection URLs out of output; then lower the byte and goroutine budget and preserve rejection evidence. Browser code uses pure values and state transitions only. amqp091-go, AMQP sockets, RabbitMQ, TLS, partitions, restarts, Docker, databases, race and leak checks, load, upgrades, and production behavior require explicit authorized transfer gates.

Before reading, predict which action preserves the Recovery Order Generation contract for the supplied data.
